issues.message: Triple object type 'event' may be incompatible with expected range 'MachineComponent' for relation 'causes'.
Triple subject type 'entity' may be incompatible with expected domain 'AlarmEvent' for relation 'causes'.
Triple object type 'event' may be incompatible with expected range 'MachineComponent' for relation 'causes'.

notes: Inferred graph initialized from validated candidate triples.

inferred_triples.predicate_label: alarms and messages of the CNC
inferred_triples.justification: The text indicates that the alarms listed (including ActiveEmergencyEvent and Open side guard alarm) are part of the category "alarms and messages of the CNC", implying a grouping relation between them.
The text states that an alarm can be recognized with the key 'RESET PRG', which triggers a program rewind event.

inferred_triples.subject_label: THERMOMAGNET SWITCHES. END OF CYCLE STOP
inferred_triples.predicate_label: THERMOMAGNET SWITCHES. END OF CYCLE STOP
inferred_triples.object_label: Check and reset circuit breakers and/or thermomagnetic switches
inferred_triples.justification: The text states that the alarm THERMOMAGNET SWITCHES. END OF CYCLE STOP triggers the intervention "Check and reset circuit breakers and/or thermomagnetic switches".
The text states that the ACTIVE EMERGENCY alarm triggers a program rewind (RESET PRG) when the alarm is displayed, indicating that the alarm causes the program to be rewound.

inferred_triples.predicate_label: Exit automatic mode and close the clamp
inferred_triples.object_label: Exit automatic mode and close the clamp
inferred_triples.justification: The alarm OPEN FORCEPS triggers the intervention Exit automatic mode and close the clamp.
The alarm "CLAMP OPENING FAILURE" indicates that the clamp did not open within the 5‑second limit, and the recommended intervention is to check mechanical resistance.
Each alarm entry lists its effect as "Alarm with immediate and controlled shutdown", indicating that the alarm triggers an immediate, controlled shutdown of the machine.
The text states that the alarm "waiting for excessive feed" is triggered by the device "CDS".

The chunk contains the phrase "responsible for operator/maintenance officer" immediately following an alarm description, indicating that the alarm event is responsible for the operator/maintenance officer.
The chunk contains the sentence "Tool control device no. 4 has detected tip breakage," linking the device to the failure event.

The chunk describes a device (e.g., Tool control device no. 4) that "does not work" or "has detected tip breakage", which is a failure event. This indicates the device has had a problem, matching the relation label "has_had_problem".
The cause field in the alarm description indicates that the Tool control device no. 4 does not work, which is a device failure that triggers the alarm.

inferred_triples.predicate_label: check the core of the probe
inferred_triples.justification: The chunk text states that the check event is performed on the core of the probe, which is a part of the device. Thus the relation connects the check event to the part.

inferred_triples.predicate_label: check the anomaly in the CDS operation panel
inferred_triples.justification: The alarm text explicitly instructs operators to "Check the anomaly on the CDS operator panel", which is an action performed on the device (the CDS). The relation candidate therefore links the check event to the device entity.

inferred_triples.predicate_label: check the anomaly on the CDS operator panel and consult the documentation
inferred_triples.justification: The chunk text for alarms 1070, 1071, and 1072 states that the intervention is "Check the anomaly on the CDS operator panel and consult the documentation." This indicates that the alarm event (cand_s_00002) triggers the check action (cand_s_00004).

inferred_triples.predicate_label: check the anomaly on the CDS operator panel and consult the documentation. If there is no sign, check the connections with the tower.
inferred_triples.justification: The text states that after checking the anomaly on the CDS operator panel, the operator consults the documentation, indicating a triggering relationship.

inferred_triples.predicate_label: check the anomaly on the CDS operator panel and consult the documentation. If there is no sign, check the connections with the lathe.
inferred_triples.justification: The alarm 1070 triggers the intervention steps described in the relation candidate: checking the anomaly on the CDS operator panel, consulting documentation, and checking connections with the lathe.

inferred_triples.predicate_label: intervention: Check the anomaly in the CDS operation panel and consult the documentation. If there is no sign, check the connections with the lathe.
inferred_triples.justification: The chunk text for Alarm 1070 states that the alarm triggers the intervention to check the anomaly on the CDS operator panel, which is a check event.

inferred_general_axioms.literal_value: An alarm type that delivers messages to operators indicating a condition without triggering a change in machine status.
An electrical safety device that interrupts current when a set temperature is reached, and can be reset manually or automatically.
An electrical safety device that interrupts overcurrent to protect equipment and prevent fire, and can be reset manually or automatically.
A thermally activated magnetic switch used as a temperature sensor and circuit interrupter in industrial machinery, typically reset during maintenance.
A digital computer used for automation of electromechanical processes, controlling machinery in industrial settings.

A fluid or liquid substance used to transfer heat away from a system, typically with high thermal capacity, low viscosity, and chemical inertness, employed in industrial processing and machine cooling.
A mechanical device that compresses gases by reducing their volume, thereby increasing pressure (and temperature if not intercoolled).
A device that creates airflow for cooling purposes in industrial equipment.
A unique identifier that distinguishes a specific alarm instance within a manufacturing system, typically expressed as a numeric or alphanumeric code.
A mechanical component that provides rotational or translational motion, such as an axis or pivot point.
An obstruction that blocks or hinders progress, often causing a machine axis to fail to reach its intended position. Obstacles can be physical or abstract and act as constraints on machine operation.
A material or substance supplied to a machine or animal to sustain operation or nutrition, commonly used as a manufacturing resource.
A G‑code command used in CNC machine control, typically referenced in feed alarms.
An event where a machine program is reversed to a previous state, typically as part of a reset operation.
An alarm event indicating an active emergency condition in a manufacturing system.
An alarm event indicating that the side guards of a machine or production line are open, creating a safety hazard for operators and potentially disrupting the production process.
An event representing the activation or initiation of a component, system, or personnel action, typically triggered by an alarm or control signal.
The state in which a machine operates as intended, without alarms or faults, representing the desired operational condition.
An event that releases pressure in a pneumatic system, typically used to disengage or reset a brake mechanism.
A machine state event representing the intended or target position of a component, such as a spindle, that the system aims to achieve during operation.
An alarm indicating that forceps are in an open state, typically triggered by a sensor detecting the open position.
An intervention event where a technician inspects, measures, or verifies the mechanical resistance of a component or system as part of routine maintenance or fault diagnosis.
Relates a CNC machine (Feature of Interest) to the alarms and messages it generates, representing abnormal operating conditions, faults, or required actions.
Relates a PLC interface (resource) to the alarm messages or alerts it generates, indicating abnormal conditions or events within a manufacturing system.
Relates an alarm, result, or sensor to the entity that issues or generates it.
Relates an alarm or abnormal condition event to the monitoring system (e.g., PLC, sensor) that identifies or senses it.
Relates an operator action, such as pressing a designated key, to the reset of a program or process.
Relates a manufacturing process to the condition that it can be restarted only when the underlying causes of failure are no longer present.
Relates a human-initiated punch button press to the alarm event it triggers.
Relates an alarm or event to a specific page and input within an electrical diagram used for troubleshooting.
Relates an alarm or emitted signal to the personnel responsible for handling it.
Relates a resource such as a document, diagram, or page to a feature of interest or specific reference point within that resource.
Relates an alarm event to a shutdown event that is initiated at the conclusion of a production cycle.
Relates an alarm to a machine or system that triggers an immediate and controlled shutdown.
Relates a source event or entity (e.g., an alarm, sensor reading, or failure) to the resulting effect or disabling of machine components.
